subject.name: Mathematics
subject.description: Mathematical foundations for computing, AI, and data systems.

topic.name: Linear Algebra
topic.description: Vectors, matrices, transformations, and the math behind modern AI.

course.title: Linear Algebra Basics
course.description: Study vectors, matrices, systems of equations, and transformations with examples connected to computing and AI.
learnObjectives: Use vectors and matrices to represent data
Solve basic systems of linear equations
Connect linear transformations to practical computing examples

modules.name: Vectors and Matrices
modules.description: Learn the basic objects of linear algebra and how they represent data.

lessons.name: Vectors as Data
lessons.description: Understand vectors as ordered lists of values with direction and magnitude.

content.overview: Vectors are a compact way to represent points, features, and directions.
content.body:
A vector can represent a point in space, an image embedding, a product profile, or the features of a house.

Operations such as addition and scaling help transform and compare these representations.

lessons.name: Matrix Operations
lessons.description: Learn how matrices organize numbers and transform vectors.

content.overview: Matrices can store structured data and represent transformations.
content.body:
A matrix is a table of numbers. In computing, matrices appear in graphics, neural networks, optimization, and data analysis.

Matrix multiplication is especially important because it composes transformations.

modules.name: Linear Systems
modules.description: Represent and solve systems of equations with matrix notation.

lessons.name: Systems in Matrix Form
lessons.description: Convert equations into AX = B notation.

content.overview: Matrix notation gives a compact way to represent many equations at once.
content.body:
A system of linear equations can be written as AX = B, where A stores coefficients, X stores unknowns, and B stores outputs.

This form lets computers solve large systems efficiently.
